title: Lola 外企职场英语课程 第一课

examples.en: The building work was carried out by a local contractor
examples.zh: 建筑工程由当地承包商进行。

examples.en: We need to carry out this work as soon as possible
examples.zh: 我们需要尽快开展这项工作。

examples.en: I just took over the job
examples.zh: 我刚接管这项工作。

examples.en: You should take over the business
examples.zh: 你应该来接管公司。

phrases.definition_zh: 开始认真工作，专注于工作

examples.en: I need to knuckle down and finish this project before the deadline
examples.zh: 我需要集中精力完成这个项目，赶在截止日期之前。

examples.en: We all need to knuckle down and work together to meet our targets this quarter
examples.zh: 我们所有人都需要集中精力并共同努力，以实现本季度的目标。

examples.en: Don't forget to hand in your report to the manager by Friday
examples.zh: 不要忘记在周五之前将你的报告交给经理。

examples.en: I need to hand in my resignation letter today because I found a new job
examples.zh: 我今天需要提交辞职信，因为我找到了一份新工作。

phrases.definition_zh: 结束工作，下班; 收工

examples.en: Let's knock off early tonight
examples.zh: 我们今晚早点收工吧。

examples.en: Let's knock off for lunch
examples.zh: 咱们收工吃午饭吧。

phrases.definition_zh: 引起注意，提出值得注意的地方

examples.en: I want to call out this problem for all of you
examples.zh: 我想让大家注意到这个问题。

examples.en: I have to call out the risk of this project
examples.zh: 我想提出这个项目的风险。

examples.en: We had to call off the meeting because the client canceled at the last minute
examples.zh: 我们不得不取消会议，因为客户在最后一刻取消了。

examples.en: The construction company called off the project due to budget constraints
examples.zh: 建筑公司因预算限制取消了该项目。

phrases.definition_zh: 如期发生; 进展顺利；奏效；行得通

examples.en: The solution didn't work out
examples.zh: 这个解决办法行不通.

examples.en: Things have worked out for us
examples.zh: 事情对我们来说进展顺利.

examples.en: Now that we have agreed on the details I'll draw up the contract and send it to you tomorrow
examples.zh: 既然我们已经就细节达成一致，我会起草合同并在明天发送给你。

phrases.definition_zh: 扩大范围; 拓展领域

examples.en: Our company wants to branch out the business
examples.zh: 我们的公司想扩大业务范围。

examples.en: The company has decided to branch out and offer new services to their customers
examples.zh: 该公司决定扩展业务并向客户提供新的服务。

examples.en: you should weigh up the consequence before making the decision
examples.zh: 你应当权衡一下后果再做这个决定。

examples.en: The manager is currently weighing up the costs and benefits of implementing a new software system
examples.zh: 经理正在权衡实施新软件系统的成本和收益。

examples.en: There is still a risk that the whole deal will fall through
examples.zh: 整桩买卖化为泡影的风险仍然存在。

examples.en: The project was supposed to be completed by the end of the month but it fell through because the team couldn't meet the deadline
examples.zh: 该项目本应该在月底完成，但由于团队无法满足截止日期，计划失败了。

examples.en: I'd like to wind up the meeting by 11 am
examples.zh: 我想在11点前完成会议。

examples.en: I need to wind up this project before I can start working on the next one
examples.zh: 我需要结束这个项目，然后才能开始下一个项目的工作。

phrases.definition_zh: 仔细检查; 用心思考

examples.en: Let's just go over this process again to make sure we're all on the same page
examples.zh: 让我们再过一遍这个流程，确保大家都明白。

examples.en: Can you go over the report and check for any errors or inconsistencies
examples.zh: 你能否仔细检查报告并查找任何错误或不一致之处？

examples.en: Let's look into this market a bit further
examples.zh: 让我们再进一步考察这个市场。

examples.en: We need to look into the feasibility of expanding our business to new markets
examples.zh: 我们需要调查将业务扩展到新市场的可行性。

phrases.definition_zh: 讨论、商讨某个情况或问题

examples.en: Let's talk over the proposal and see if there are any areas that need to be revised
examples.zh: 让我们商讨一下这个提案，看看是否有需要修改的地方。

examples.en: I need to talk over my career goals with my supervisor and get some feedback
examples.zh: 我需要与我的主管商讨一下我的职业目标，并获得一些反馈。

phrases.definition_zh: 向某人描述，解释，展示某事

examples.en: For the first part of my talk I'm going to take you through last year's achievements
examples.zh: 在我演讲的第一部分，我将带你们回顾去年的成就。

examples.en: Let me take you through the new recruitment process
examples.zh: 让我带您了解新的招聘流程。

phrases.definition_zh: 接受，同意某人的提议或邀请

examples.en: I'll take you up on that. I think that's a fair proposal
examples.zh: 我接受你的提议。我觉得这是一个合理的提议。

examples.en: Sarah has really taken to her new position as sales manager
examples.zh: 已经非常适应销售经理的新职位。

examples.en: How have you taken to your new role as the group leader
examples.zh: 你对组长的新角色适应得怎样？

phrases.definition_zh: 承担，接受（尤指艰巨工作或重大责任）；雇佣，录用

examples.en: She takes on greater responsibilities when she is promoted
examples.zh: 她被提升后，承担了更大的责任。

examples.en: We are looking to take on another Risk Analyst next month
examples.zh: 我们希望下个月再聘请一位风险分析师。

examples.en: The launch of our new product line has really taken off
examples.zh: 我们新产品线的推出真的很成功。

examples.en: Since early last year venture capital investments in blockchain technology have really taken off
examples.zh: 自去年年初以来，区块链技术的风险投资开始迅速发展.

phrases.definition_zh: 把人带到一边; 单独谈话

examples.en: After the team meeting my manager took me aside to check if I had understood everything
examples.zh: 团队会议结束后，我的经理把我带到一边，确定我是否理解了一切。

examples.en: I decided to take her aside because she looked upset after my remarks
examples.zh: 我决定单独找她谈谈，因为她听了我的话后看起来很沮丧。

examples.en: I was able to take in a lot from the training because of her engaging teaching style
examples.zh: 由于她引人入胜的教学风格，我从培训中获得了很多。

examples.en: Tell me what you took in from that discussion with the board this morning
examples.zh: 告诉我你从今天早上与董事会的讨论中得到了什么。

phrases.definition_zh: 把想法或点子说清楚；使一个想法被传达，被理解，被表达

examples.en: It was a challenge to get across my idea to the CEO but I succeeded in the end
examples.zh: 把我的想法传达给首席执行官是一个挑战，但最终我成功了。

examples.en: what is the message that you are trying to get across
examples.zh: 你想传达的信息是什么？

phrases.definition_zh: 开始认真做，开始认真处理、对待

examples.en: Let's get down to the specifics
examples.zh: 让我们来看看细节。

examples.en: Now that everyone has met, let's get down to business
examples.zh: 现在大家都互相认识了，我们就进入正题吧。

examples.en: After taking on this extra responsibility I have got behind with sales reports
examples.zh: 在承担了这个额外的工作之后，我的销售报告进度就落后了。

examples.en: Our company is starting to get into other types of technologies such as AI and blockchain
examples.zh: 我们的公司开始涉足其他类型的技术，比如如人工智能和区块链领域。

phrases.definition_zh: 偏离；跑题，说和主题无关的事情

examples.en: You're getting away from the point. We should be focusing on next year's budget
examples.zh: 你偏离主题了。我们应该专注在明年的预算上。

examples.en: Let's get back to discussing the main topic of the meeting
examples.zh: 让我们回到讨论会议的主要议题上。

examples.en: I'm sorry I can't talk right now but I'll get back to you as soon as possible
examples.zh: 对不起，我现在不能说话，但我会尽快回复你。

phrases.definition_zh: 恢复过来；克服困难，问题

examples.en: It's probably going to take us a couple of years to get over this crisis
examples.zh: 我们可能需要几年时间才能从这次危机中恢复过来。

phrases.definition_zh: 完成难做的任务或工作

examples.en: Although the training was very intensive, I got through it
examples.zh: 虽然培训强度很大，但我还是完成了。

examples.en: Let's wrap up the meeting and get back to work
examples.zh: 让我们结束会议, 继续工作吧

examples.en: I think it's time to wrap up this conversation
examples.zh: 我认为现在是结束这个谈话的时候了。

examples.en: I need to sort out my schedule before I can make plans for the weekend
examples.zh: 我需要整理一下我的日程才能制定周末计划。

examples.en: The company is working to sort out the supply chain issue
examples.zh: 公司正在努力解决供应链问题。

examples.en: Could you please chase up the payment for the invoice we sent
examples.zh: 你能跟进一下我们发出的发票的付款情况吗？

examples.en: The project is behind schedule so we need to chase up the progress
examples.zh: 这个项目进度拖延了，所以我们需要跟进一下进展情况。

examples.en: Don't slack off just because it's Friday afternoon We still have work to do
examples.zh: 别因为是星期五下午就懒散起来。我们还有工作要做。

examples.en: He got fired because he was always slacking off and not doing his job
examples.zh: 他被解雇了，因为他总是偷懒，没有认真做好自己的工作。

examples.en: The paperwork is starting to pile up on my desk
examples.zh: 文书工作开始在我的桌子上积累起来.

examples.en: If we don't address the issue now it will pile up and become a bigger problem later on
examples.zh: 如果我们现在不解决问题，它会积累起来，以后会变成更大的问题。

examples.en: We still need to iron out a few issues
examples.zh: 我们仍需要解决一些问题。

examples.en: We need to iron out the details of the contract before we can sign it
examples.zh: 我们需要敲定合同的细节，然后才能签署。

phrases.definition_zh: 奏效；取得成功；取得进展

examples.en: I'm not sure if this idea will pan out but it's worth a try
examples.zh: 我不确定这个点子是否会奏效，但是值得一试。

examples.en: I'm hoping this interview will pan out and I'll get the job
examples.zh: 我希望这次面试能够成功，我能得到这份工作。

phrases.definition_zh: 利用；借助；依靠；意为通过某种手段或资源来实现某个目标，或者通过利用某种优势来获得更大的好处或效益。

examples.en: We need to leverage on our marketing campaign to increase sales
examples.zh: 我们需要利用我们的营销活动来增加销售额。

examples.en: She is able to leverage on her connections to secure a job interview
examples.zh: 她能够利用她的人脉关系来获得一次面试机会。

phrases.definition_zh: 推出； 可以表示推出新产品，新服务，系统等

examples.en: The company is planning to roll out its new product line next month
examples.zh: 公司计划在下个月推出新的产品线。

examples.en: To avoid issues we need to test before rolling out the new system
examples.zh: 为避免问题，我们需要在推出新系统之前进行测试。

phrases.definition_zh: 让某人参与其中; 让某人参与进来

examples.en: Let's loop in the project manager to discuss the timeline and budget
examples.zh: 让我们拉上项目经理一起讨论时间表和预算。

examples.en: I'll loop in our tech support team to help troubleshoot the issue
examples.zh: 我会拉上我们的技术支持团队来帮助解决这个问题。

phrases.definition_zh: 与...建立密切关系；加强与...的联系

examples.en: Working on a project together really helped us bond with each other
examples.zh: 一起完成一个项目让我们彼此建立了密切关系。

examples.en: Regular social events can help you bond with your clients
examples.zh: 常规的社交活动有助于加强与客户的联系。

phrases.definition_zh: 回到某一点上；回到某个话题

examples.en: Let's circle back later
examples.zh: 我们稍后再回来。

examples.en: Can we circle back to that point
examples.zh: 我们能回到那个点吗？

phrases.definition_zh: 涉足；打入；它通常表示进入新的领域或市场并取得成功

examples.en: We need to come up with a new marketing strategy to break into the Asian market
examples.zh: 我们需要想出一种新的市场营销策略，以进入亚洲市场。

examples.en: The new product is an attempt to break into the luxury goods market
examples.zh: 这个新产品是试图进入奢侈品市场的一次尝试。

examples.en: We need to scale up our team to meet the project deadline
examples.zh: 我们需要扩大团队以满足项目截止日期。

examples.en: Our sales have been growing so we need to scale up our production
examples.zh: 我们的销售一直在增长，所以我们需要扩大生产。

examples.en: Let's hold off until we have more information before making a decision
examples.zh: 在我们获得更多信息之前，让我们暂停做出决定。

examples.en: The market is too unpredictable right now so let's hold off for now
examples.zh: 现在市场太不可预测了，所以我们先暂缓一下。

phrases.definition_zh: 逐步淘汰. 用于指逐步淘汰某个产品或服务

examples.en: The company plans to phase out its older model of smartphone and focus on the new ones
examples.zh: 公司计划淘汰旧款的智能手机，专注在新款上。

examples.en: The department is phasing out the use of outdated software
examples.zh: 该部门正在逐步淘汰过时的软件.

phrases.definition_zh: 温习，复习，指回顾某个技能或知识

examples.en: I need to brush up on my Excel skills before the presentation next week
examples.zh: 下周演示之前，我需要复习一下我的Excel技能。

examples.en: I'm planning to brush up on my language skills before I travel to Europe for the business trip
examples.zh: 我计划在出差前复习一下我的语言技能，因为要去欧洲。

examples.en: I'll swing by your desk and pickup the report before the meeting
examples.zh: 我会顺便经过你的办公桌上取报告然后去开会。

examples.en: Can you swing by the client's office and drop off the proposal
examples.zh: 你能顺便到客户办公室去把提案交上去吗？

examples.en: Don't forget to clock in when you arrive at the office
examples.zh: 到达办公室时不要忘记打卡。

examples.en: I clocked in at 9 am this morning and worked until 5 pm
examples.zh: 我今天早上9点打卡开始工作，一直工作到下午5点。

examples.en: I usually clock in at 8 am and clock off at 5 pm
examples.zh: 我通常在早上8点打卡上班，在下午5点打卡下班。

examples.en: I usually clock off at 6 pm, but today I need to work late to finish this report
examples.zh: 我通常下午6点打卡下班，但今天我需要加班完成这份报告。

examples.en: Don't forget to clock off before you leave the office, otherwise the system won't record your working hours
examples.zh: 离开办公室前别忘了打卡下班，否则系统不会记录你的工作时间。

examples.en: I'm looking forward to getting off work so I can relax
examples.zh: 我期待下班后能够放松。

examples.en: We are not allowed to get off early today because we have a lot of work to do
examples.zh: 今天我们不能提前下班，因为我们还有很多工作要做。

title: Lola 外企职场英语课程 第二课

phrases.definition_zh: 艰苦地工作；苦干；努力奋斗；痛苦地坚持做某事；努力地工作或坚持不懈地做某件事情，通常是指在艰难或乏味的工作中持续投入努力。

examples.en: The team has been slogging away for weeks to prepare for the upcoming conference
examples.zh: 团队已经苦干了几周，为即将到来的会议做准备。

examples.en: The team slogged away for months to launch the new product
examples.zh: 团队为推出新产品苦干了数月。

examples.en: We slogged away all night to prepare for the presentation
examples.zh: 我们整夜苦干，为演示做准备。

phrases.definition_zh: 坚持不懈地工作；不停地努力

examples.en: The team is plugging away to meet the deadline for the project
examples.zh: 团队正在不断努力，以满足项目的截止日期。

examples.en: He's been plugging away at this business for years and now it's finally starting to pay off
examples.zh: 他多年来一直在坚持经营他的生意，现在终于开始有所回报了。

examples.en: I'm going to take off next Friday to attend a family event
examples.zh: 我打算下周五请假参加家庭活动。

examples.en: The company allows us to take off two weeks for vacation every year
examples.zh: 公司每年允许我们休两周的假。

examples.en: Due to the challenging economic situation, the company is considering selling off some of its unnecessary assets
examples.zh: 由于经济形势严峻，公司正在考虑出售一些不必要的资产。

examples.en: The massive sell-off in the stock market caused a significant drop in share prices for many companies
examples.zh: 股票市场上的大规模抛售导致了很多公司的股票价格的大幅下跌。

examples.en: Before we can officially implement this plan we need to have the company leadership sign off on it
examples.zh: 在我们正式实施这个计划之前，我们需要让公司领导签署批准。

examples.en: He's the primary investor in this project and needs to sign off on our budget plan before we can proceed
examples.zh: 他是这个项目的主要投资者，他需要先批准我们的预算计划才能继续进行。

examples.en: Due to the large workload we decided to farm out some parts of the project to other companies
examples.zh: 由于工作量太大，我们决定把这个项目的某些部分外包给其他公司。

examples.en: We don't have enough resources to complete this project so we need to farm it out to other companies
examples.zh: 我们没有足够的资源来完成这个项目，因此我们需要找其他公司来帮忙。

examples.en: Due to poor company performance they were forced to lay off employees
examples.zh: 由于公司业绩不佳，他们被迫裁员了。

examples.en: They laid off 50 of their staff to cope with the economic downturn
examples.zh: 他们裁掉了50%的工作人员，以应对经济不景气的情况。

examples.en: I need to turn over the project to the new manager by the end of the week
examples.zh: 我需要在本周末将这个项目移交给新的经理。

examples.en: They turned over the financial report to the new auditor
examples.zh: 他们把财务报告移交给了新的审计师。

examples.en: The store was able to turn over all of its inventory during the holiday season
examples.zh: 这家商店在节日销售季节里成功售出了所有库存。

examples.en: Marco's company is doing well. It turns over almost 250,000 a year
examples.zh: Marco的公司运营良好，它每年的营业额接近25万美元。

phrases.definition_zh: 清楚地展示；清楚地解释

examples.en: At the start of your presentation lay out the main points you are going to discuss
examples.zh: 在你的演示开始时，先列出你将要讨论的主要观点。

examples.en: The HR team will lay out the job responsibilities and expectations for the new hire
examples.zh: 人力资源团队将说明新员工的工作职责和期望。

phrases.definition_zh: 预留时间；暂定计划；暂定安排；暂时的计划或安排，这些计划可能会在以后被更改或确认。

examples.en: I might be busy tomorrow but let's pencil in a meeting anyway
examples.zh: 我明天可能很忙，但是我们还是把会议安排在明天吧

examples.en: I'll have a meeting tomorrow. Let's pencil in sometime to review the proposal
examples.zh: 我们明天预留一些时间来过一遍方案。

examples.en: Our company has fallen behind our competitors in terms of innovation
examples.zh: 我们公司在创新方面落后于我们的竞争对手。

examples.en: The team's productivity has fallen behind due to lack of motivation
examples.zh: 由于缺乏动力，团队的生产力已经落后。

examples.en: Can you please elaborate on how the new system will improve our work flow
examples.zh: 你能详细说明一下新系统将如何改善我们的工作流程吗？

examples.en: The manager asked me to elaborate on my sales projections during the meeting
examples.zh: 经理要求我在会议上详细说明我的销售预测。

examples.en: The company is looking to cash in on the growing demand for eco friendly products
examples.zh: 公司正在寻求从对环保产品的不断增长的需求中获利。

examples.en: The marketing team was able to successfully cash in on the latest social media trend
examples.zh: 营销团队能够成功利用最新的社交媒体趋势获利。

phrases.definition_zh: 涉足；打入；它通常表示进入新的领域或市场并取得成功

examples.en: We need to come up with a new marketing strategy to break into the Asian market
examples.zh: 我们需要想出一种新的市场营销策略，以进入亚洲市场。

examples.en: The new product is an attempt to break into the luxury goods market
examples.zh: 这个新产品是试图进入奢侈品市场的一次尝试。

examples.en: We need to scale up our team to meet the project deadline
examples.zh: 我们需要扩大团队以满足项目截止日期。

examples.en: Our sales have been growing so we need to scale up our production
examples.zh: 我们的销售一直在增长，所以我们需要扩大生产。

phrases.definition_zh: 弄清楚、明确或确认某事物；通常指需要努力调查、研究或讨论才能得出结论或明确答案。

examples.en: We must pin down the goals and deadlines for this task
examples.zh: 我们需要弄清楚这个任务的目标和期限。

examples.en: We need to pin down the actual requirements for this project
examples.zh: 我们需要弄清楚这个项目的实际需求。

phrases.definition_zh: 加大努力；加强投入；需要更加努力、投入更多的时间、精力、资源等来解决问题，以期实现目标

examples.en: Our team needs to double down our efforts to improve customer satisfaction
examples.zh: 我们的团队需要加倍努力，提高客户满意度。

examples.en: The company needs to double down its marketing strategy to expand its customer base
examples.zh: 公司需要加倍投入营销策略，扩大客户群。

phrases.definition_zh: 充实；完善；它通常指通过添加更多的细节或信息来完善一个想法、计划，以使其更具可操作性或可实现性。我们需要充实这个想法，使它成为一个可行的商业计划。

examples.en: Please flesh out your ideas at the meeting so that we can better understand your perspective
examples.zh: 请在会议上充实你的想法，这样我们就可以更好地理解你的观点。

examples.en: They are fleshing out their business plan to show the investors more potential
examples.zh: 他们正在充实他们的商业计划，以便向投资者展示更多的潜力。

phrases.definition_zh: 到达某个结论、意识到某个事实或意识到某种情况的状态。

examples.en: We eventually came to the conclusion that the plan was not feasible
examples.zh: 最终我们得出结论，该计划不可行。

examples.en: After a discussion, we finally came to the realization of our mistake
examples.zh: 经过一番讨论，我们最终认识到我们的错误。

phrases.definition_zh: 加强、加大、提高；来表示增加某个方面的生产、产量、效率、力度、规模等，或提高某个方面的水平、质量、能力等。

examples.en: We need to ramp up production to meet the increasing demand for our product
examples.zh: 我们需要加快生产速度，以满足对我们产品不断增长的需求。

examples.en: The new project requires us to ramp up our research and development efforts
examples.zh: 这个新项目要求我们加大研究和开发的力度。

examples.en: We're going to ramp up our marketing campaign to promote our new product launch
examples.zh: 我们将加大营销活动力度，以推广我们的新产品上市。

phrases.definition_zh: 深入了解、深入挖掘，深入分析

examples.en: We need to drill down into the features and benefits of the product
examples.zh: 我们需要深入了解产品的功能和优点。

examples.en: We need to drill down on the sales data to develop better sales strategies
examples.zh: 我们需要对销售数据进行详细分析，以制定更好的销售策略。

examples.en: What's holding you back from completing the project on time
examples.zh: 是什么妨碍了你按时完成项目？

examples.en: The lack of communication is holding back our team's progress
examples.zh: 缺乏沟通阻碍了我们团队的进展。

phrases.definition_zh: 给出意见；提供支持；强调给出有分量的、有价值的意见或支持

examples.en: Can you weigh in with your thoughts on this issue
examples.zh: 你能给出你对这个问题的看法吗？

examples.en: Let's wait for the boss to weigh in before we finalize our plans
examples.zh: 在我们最终确定计划之前，让我们等待老板发表意见。

phrases.definition_zh: 发表意见、提出反馈；强调某人愿意向上级、同事或在工作场合中公开提出个人的反馈和意见

examples.en: Don't be afraid to voice out your ideas and opinions during the brainstorming session
examples.zh: 在头脑风暴会议中，不要害怕表达你的想法和观点。

examples.en: I finally voiced out my concerns to my manager
examples.zh: 我终于鼓起勇气向我的经理表达了我的担忧。

examples.en: His experience and qualifications really make him stand out
examples.zh: 他的经验和资格真的让他脱颖而出。

examples.en: This design stands out from the others we've seen
examples.zh: 这个设计与我们看到的其他设计截然不同。

phrases.definition_zh: 在...基础上扩展、发展、建立

examples.en: We can build up on this idea to create a better plan
examples.zh: 我们可以在这个想法的基础上制定一个更好的计划。

examples.en: We can build upon our previous success and expand our business to new markets
examples.zh: 我们可以在以前的成功基础上，将业务拓展到新的市场。

phrases.definition_zh: 敲定、制定、达成、解决、协商、完成

examples.en: We need to hammer out a budget before we can proceed
examples.zh: 在我们继续之前，我们需要制定一个预算。

examples.en: The team is meeting to hammer out the details of the proposal
examples.zh: 团队正在开会敲定该提案的细节。

phrases.definition_zh: 成功完成；实现目标；它通常用来表示需要克服困难来完成一个具有一些难度或挑战性的任务或目标

examples.en: It was a difficult task but he managed to pull it off
examples.zh: 这是一项艰巨的任务，但他设法完成了。

examples.en: Despite the challenges, he was able to pull off a great event
examples.zh: 尽管面临挑战，他还是成功举办了一场精彩的活动。

phrases.definition_zh: 达到;符合合期望或要求

examples.en: Does his work measure up to our standards
examples.zh: 他的工作是否达到我们的标准？

examples.en: We need to make sure our team can measure up to the competition
examples.zh: 我们需要确保我们的团队能够达到竞争对手的水平。

examples.en: I'm not sure if I can measure up to the expectations of the new position
examples.zh: 我不确定我能否达到新职位的期望。

examples.en: It's a difficult concept to explain but once you catch on it all makes sense
examples.zh: 这是一个很难解释的概念，但一旦你理解了，一切就都有意义了。

examples.en: It took me a little while to catch on to the new software system but now I find it much easier to use
examples.zh: 我花了一点时间才理解这个新的软件系统，但现在我觉得使用起来更容易了。

phrases.definition_zh: 创造或开辟；创造或开辟出一部分资源、时间、市场份额等。短语经常用于描述人们在工作中努力争取和获取某些东西的过程。

examples.en: The company is trying to carve out a larger share of the market by introducing new products
examples.zh: 公司试图通过推出新产品开拓更大的市场份额。

examples.en: I need to carve out sometime this week to work on the project
examples.zh: 我需要在这周内抽出一些时间来工作这个项目。

examples.en: She carved out a few hours every weekend to work on her side business
examples.zh: 她每个周末腾出几个小时来做她的副业。

examples.en: Let's team up to finish this project on time
examples.zh: 让我们联合起来，按时完成这个项目。

examples.en: Our company is teaming up with a leading tech company to develop new software
examples.zh: 我们公司正在与一家领先的科技公司合作开发新软件。

phrases.definition_zh: 快速过一遍; 快速查看

examples.en: I'll quickly run through the process for you
examples.zh: 我会快速给你介绍一下流程。

examples.en: Can you run through the main points of the proposal
examples.zh: 你能过一遍提案的主要要点吗？

phrases.definition_zh: 推进；继续前进；继续进行

examples.en: Let's put that mistake behind us and move forward
examples.zh: 让我们不要纠结这个错误，继续往前吧

examples.en: After the meeting we can move forward to the next phase of the plan
examples.zh: 会议结束后，我们可以继续进入计划的下一个阶段。

examples.en: The company's innovative approach brought about a significant increase in sales
examples.zh: 公司的创新方法使得销售有了显著增长。

examples.en: We need to work together to bring about a more efficient process
examples.zh: 我们需要共同努力，实现更高效的流程。

examples.en: Can I bring up a concern I have about the new project proposal
examples.zh: 我可以提出我对新项目提案的担忧吗？

examples.en: She brought up some good points in the brainstorming session
examples.zh: 在头脑风暴会议上，她提出了一些好的观点。

phrases.definition_zh: 导致；引起；致使；该短语表示某种因素、行动或措施导致了某种结果或者后果

examples.en: Poor communication can lead to misunderstandings and mistakes
examples.zh: 沟通不畅可能导致误解和错误。

examples.en: Poor time management can lead to missed deadlines and project delays
examples.zh: 时间管理不当可能导致错过截止日期和项目延迟。

phrases.definition_zh: 适应；融入；相协调；相匹配

examples.en: We'll need to modify the design to fit in with the client's requirements
examples.zh: 我们需要修改设计以适应客户的要求。

examples.en: She was able to fit in with the team quickly
examples.zh: 她可以很快的就融入团队。

examples.en: We need to find a way to create content that resonates with our target client
examples.zh: 我们需要找到一种方法，创造出能与目标客户产生共鸣的内容。

examples.en: Her presentation really resonated with the audience and received a lot of positive feedback
examples.zh: 她的演讲非常能与听众产生共鸣，并获得了很多积极反馈。

phrases.definition_zh: 与..相协调，相匹配，相一致

examples.en: our team needs to align with the client's expectations
examples.zh: 我们的团队需要与客户的期望相一致.

examples.en: our marketing campaign needs to align with the target audience's interests and needs
examples.zh: 我们的营销活动需要与目标受众的兴趣和需求相匹配.

phrases.definition_zh: 插话；插嘴；别人说话时加入自己的发言，积极参与并与他人分享自己的想法和观点。

examples.en: Feel free to chime in with your thoughts during the meeting
examples.zh: 在会议期间可以随时发表你的想法。

examples.en: When discussing the budget, everyone should be able to chime in with their opinions
examples.zh: 讨论预算时，每个人都应该能够发表自己的意见。

examples.en: Let's sync up later this week to go over the project timeline
examples.zh: 本周晚些时候让我们同步一下，过一遍项目时间表。

examples.en: I'll sync up with the IT department to see if they can provide the data we need
examples.zh: 我会与IT部门同步一下，看看他们是否能够提供我们需要的数据。

examples.en: Don't leave out any important details when writing the report
examples.zh: 写报告时不要遗漏任何重要细节。

examples.en: We cannot leave out any potential risks in the risk assessment report
examples.zh: 在风险评估报告中不能忽略任何潜在风险。

examples.en: I think we should leave out the irrelevant information from the presentation
examples.zh: 我认为我们应该在演示中省略无关的信息。

title: Lola 外企职场英语课程 第三课

examples.en: She will take over the project management from me starting next week
examples.zh: 她从下周开始将接替我负责项目管理。

examples.en: The new CEO will take over the company's operations in three months
examples.zh: 新任CEO将在三个月后接管公司的运营。

examples.en: The department is trying to line up a budget for the next quarter
examples.zh: 部门正在努力为下一个季度安排预算。

examples.en: Please line up the documents in order of importance
examples.zh: 请按照重要性将文件整理好。

examples.en: Let's break down the costs of this project and see where we can cut expenses
examples.zh: 让我们分解一下这个项目的成本，看看我们能在哪里削减支出。

examples.en: The project manager asked me to break down the tasks into smaller pieces
examples.zh: 项目经理要求我将任务分解成小块。

examples.en: The idea is still in the brainstorming stage but we expect it to take form in the coming weeks
examples.zh: 这个想法目前还处于头脑风暴阶段，但我们预计它将在未来几周中形成。

examples.en: The team is taking form and we're excited to get started
examples.zh: 团队正在形成，我们很兴奋地开始工作。

examples.en: The strategy for entering a new market is starting to take form
examples.zh: 进入新市场的战略开始形成。

examples.en: We need to scale back on expenses this quarter
examples.zh: 我们这个季度需要缩减开支。

examples.en: The original plan was too complex we decided to scale back and simplify it
examples.zh: 最初的计划过于复杂，我们决定缩减并简化它。

examples.en: We're scaling back the size of the team to improve efficiency
examples.zh: 我们正在缩减团队规模以提高效率。

examples.en: Can I cut in here and add my perspective to the discussion
examples.zh: 我可以打扰一下并加入讨论，提出我的观点吗？

examples.en: Sorry to cut in but we need to discuss the budget before we can move forward with the project
examples.zh: 抱歉打扰一下，但我们需要讨论预算，然后才能继续推进项目。

examples.en: The company is racing against competitors to launch its new product in the market
examples.zh: 公司正在与竞争对手比拼，争取在市场推出新产品。

examples.en: We're racing against the clock to finish this project before the deadline
examples.zh: 我们正在争分夺秒地完成这个项目，在截止日期之前。

examples.en: We need to size up the company's financial situation before deciding whether to expand the business
examples.zh: 在决定是否扩张业务之前，我们需要评估公司的财务状况。

examples.en: Before starting the project we need to carefully size up the risks and potential issues
examples.zh: 在开始项目之前，我们需要仔细评估风险和潜在的问题。

examples.en: She talked her boss into giving her a raise
examples.zh: 她说服了老板给她加薪。

examples.en: The sales manager talked the client into increasing their order quantity
examples.zh: 销售经理说服客户增加订单数量。

examples.en: We need to turnaround the company's financial performance this quarter
examples.zh: 我们需要在这个季度扭转公司的财务表现。

examples.en: The company was able to turn around its reputation with a successful public relations campaign
examples.zh: 通过一次成功的公关活动，公司成功扭转了其声誉。

examples.en: The company will parcel out the marketing budget to different campaigns
examples.zh: 该公司将把市场营销预算分配给各种市场活动。

examples.en: The HR manager will parcel out the employee benefits according to the company policy
examples.zh: 人力资源经理将根据公司政策分配员工福利。

examples.en: The manager tried to smooth over the conflict between two team members
examples.zh: 经理试图化解两个团队成员之间的冲突。

examples.en: She was able to smooth over the situation with a quick apology
examples.zh: 她通过快速道歉化解了局面。

phrases.definition_zh: 赢得......的支持；争取......的好感

examples.en: The marketing team was able to win over the target audience with a creative advertising campaign
examples.zh: 市场团队通过一次有创意的广告活动赢得了目标受众的支持。

examples.en: The new manager quickly won over her team by demonstrating her expertise
examples.zh: 新经理通过展示她的专业知识迅速获得了团队的支持。

examples.en: Before starting the project we need to map out a clear plan and timeline
examples.zh: 在开始项目之前，我们需要制定一个清晰的计划和时间表。

examples.en: The marketing team will map out the advertising strategy for the new product launch
examples.zh: 市场团队将制定新产品发布的广告策略。

examples.en: Please don't interfere with my decision making process
examples.zh: 请不要干扰我的决策过程。

examples.en: I don't want to interfere with your work flow but have you considered trying this new software
examples.zh: 我不想干扰你的工作流程，但是你考虑过尝试这个新软件吗？

examples.en: Our marketing team uses social media to interact with customers and promote our brand
examples.zh: 我们的营销团队使用社交媒体与客户互动，推广我们的品牌。

examples.en: The manager needs to interact with their teams to keep everyone aligned and motivated
examples.zh: 经理需要与他们的团队互动，以保持每个人的一致性和动力。

phrases.definition_zh: 全力以赴; 专注工作

examples.en: Let's buckle down and get this project done before the deadline
examples.zh: 让我们专心工作，在截止日期前完成工作。

examples.en: The team needs to buckle down and focus on meeting their sales targets
examples.zh: 团队需要集中精力，专注于实现销售目标。

examples.en: Networking is a great way to get ahead in your career
examples.zh: 人脉是在职业生涯中取得进步的一个好方法。

examples.en: If you want to get ahead in this company you need to be willing to take on more responsibility
examples.zh: 如果你想在这个公司有所发展，你需要愿意承担更多的责任。

examples.en: Let's schedule a meeting to hash out the timeline for the project
examples.zh: 让我们安排一次会议，讨论项目的时间表。

examples.en: The team is hashing out the budget
examples.zh: 团队正在讨论预算。

examples.en: I need sometime to ponder over this proposal before I give you my decision
examples.zh: 我需要一些时间来考虑这个提议，然后再给你我的决定。

examples.en: We should ponder over the pros and cons of this project before we move forward
examples.zh: 在我们继续推进这个项目之前，我们应该权衡一下它的利弊。

examples.en: We cannot veer off the original plan without consulting our manager first
examples.zh: 在未经咨询我们的经理之前，我们不能偏离原计划。

examples.en: The discussion veered off topic and we had to bring it back on track
examples.zh: 讨论偏离了话题，我们不得不将其拉回正轨。

examples.en: The company stood up to the challenge of entering a new market and succeeded
examples.zh: 公司应对进入新市场的挑战，最终成功了。

examples.en: It's time for us to stand up to the competition and innovate our product offerings
examples.zh: 是我们应对竞争，创新产品的时候了。

examples.en: The team stood up to the pressure and delivered the project ahead of schedule
examples.zh: 团队顶住压力，提前完成了项目。

examples.en: We need to rule out all possible risks before we make a final decision
examples.zh: 在做出最终决定之前，我们需要排除所有可能的风险。

examples.en: We cannot rule out the possibility of having to make budget cuts in the near future
examples.zh: 我们不能排除近期可能需要进行预算削减的可能性。

examples.en: The company decided to pour in more resources to boost sales in the new market
examples.zh: 公司决定投入更多资源来推动新市场的销售。

examples.en: We need to pour in more effort to meet our sales targets
examples.zh: 我们需要投入更多的努力来达到销售目标。

examples.en: It's important to rein in spending and stay within our budget
examples.zh: 控制开支，保持在预算范围内是非常重要的。

examples.en: The project manager had to rein in the scope of the project to meet the deadline
examples.zh: 项目经理不得不缩小项目范围以满足截止日期。

examples.en: The IT team had to rein in the use of personal devices for security reasons
examples.zh: 出于安全考虑，IT团队不得不控制个人设备的使用。

examples.en: I need to slim down my workload and delegate tasks to my team
examples.zh: 我需要减少工作量并把任务交给我的团队。

examples.en: Let's slim down the meeting agenda to focus on key topics
examples.zh: 让我们简化会议议程，专注于关键议题。

examples.en: We need to slim down our expenses during project execution to stay within budget
examples.zh: 我们需要在项目执行过程中减少开支，以保持预算内。

examples.en: The sales of our product have started to fall off in the last quarter
examples.zh: 我们产品的销售在上个季度开始下滑。

examples.en: The company's profits have been falling off
examples.zh: 公司的利润一直在下降。

examples.en: His overtime work during the busy season paid off with a promotion
examples.zh: 他在旺季的加班工作得到了回报，他获得了升职。

examples.en: The company's investment in new technology is beginning to pay off
examples.zh: 公司对新技术的投资开始见效。

examples.en: The manager asked everyone to stand aside and let the expert handle the technical issue
examples.zh: 经理要求大家让专家来处理技术问题，不要干涉。

examples.en: when there was a conflict between two departments the CEO decided to stand aside and let the department heads resolve the issue themselves
examples.zh: 当两个部门之间出现冲突时，CEO决定不干涉，让部门负责人自行解决.

examples.en: Please turn over the files to the next person in the work flow
examples.zh: 请将文件交接给工作流程中的下一个人。

examples.en: The manager decided to turn over the task to a more experienced team member
examples.zh: 经理决定将任务交接给一个更有经验的团队成员。

examples.en: He was not happy with the direction of the team and decided to pull out
examples.zh: 他不满意团队的发展方向，决定退出。

examples.en: The company is planning to pull out of the market due to the declining sales
examples.zh: 由于销售下滑，公司计划退出市场。

examples.en: We can knock off early today since we've finished all the tasks for the week
examples.zh: 我们今天可以提前下班，因为我们已经完成了本周的所有任务。

examples.en: After we knock off let's grab a drink at the bar across the street
examples.zh: 下班后，让我们到街对面的酒吧喝一杯。

examples.en: If we want to finish this project on time we need to knuckle down and work more efficiently
examples.zh: 如果我们想按时完成这个项目，我们需要认真工作并提高效率。

examples.en: The team needs to knuckle down and focus on meeting the upcoming deadline
examples.zh: 团队需要认真工作，专注于满足即将到来的截止日期。

phrases.definition_zh: 指让某个信息、观点等被理解、接受或传达

examples.en: We need to figure out how to get across this complex concept in a way that everyone can understand
examples.zh: 我们需要想办法让大家都能理解这个复杂的概念。

examples.en: Our marketing campaign needs to get across the message that our brand is innovative and trustworthy
examples.zh: 我们的营销活动需要传达出我们的品牌创新、值得信赖的信息。

phrases.definition_zh: 记下;草草写下；通常用于表示快速记录或写下一些信息或想法。

examples.en: Before you forget jot down that important task so you don't overlook it later
examples.zh: 在你忘记之前，草草地记录下那个重要任务，以便以后不会忽略它。

examples.en: I like to jot down my daily to do list every morning to help me stay organized throughout the day
examples.zh: 我喜欢每天早上记录下我的待办事项清单，以帮助我整天保持有序。

examples.en: It's important to kickoff a project with a clear understanding of the goals and timeline
examples.zh: 开始项目时，清楚地了解目标和时间表非常重要。

examples.en: Our team is planning to kickoff the marketing campaign next week
examples.zh: 我们的团队计划下周开展营销活动。

examples.en: We need to nail down the details of the contract before we can proceed
examples.zh: 我们需要确定合同的细节才能继续进行。

examples.en: The team needs to nail down the budget for the upcoming quarter
examples.zh: 团队需要确定下一个季度的预算。

phrases.definition_zh: 通常表示在某些方面进行增强和改进，以取得更好的结果或效果

examples.en: We need to juice up our marketing campaign to attract more customers
examples.zh: 我们需要提升营销活动的力度，以吸引更多的客户。

examples.en: We're going to juice up the website's design to improve user engagement
examples.zh: 我们将改进网站的设计，以提高用户参与度。

phrases.definition_zh: 好好考虑一下；指的是在做出决定之前需要仔细思考和评估

examples.en: I need to sleep on this decision before I commit to it
examples.zh: 我需要在做出决定之前好好考虑一下。

examples.en: We can't rush into this project Let's sleep on it and make sure we have a solid plan before we start
examples.zh: 我们不能匆忙开始这个项目，让我们好好考虑一下，确保我们有一个可靠的计划再开始。

examples.en: The CEO made it clear that all employees must abide by the company's dress code policy
examples.zh: CEO明确表示，所有员工必须遵守公司的着装规定。

examples.en: Everyone should abide by the confidentiality agreement to protect sensitive information
examples.zh: 每个人都必须遵守保密协议，以保护敏感信息。

phrases.definition_zh: 对...做出解释；说明...的原因；对...负责

examples.en: Can you account for the time you spent on that project last week
examples.zh: 你能解释上周你在那个项目上花费的时间吗？

examples.en: I need you to account for why the project is over budget
examples.zh: 我需要你解释为什么项目超出了预算。

examples.en: The software engineer needs to account for the bugs in the program
examples.zh: 软件工程师需要报告程序中的错误。

phrases.definition_zh: 大惊小怪；小题大做；过分在意

examples.en: The boss told us not to make a fuss over small mistakes
examples.zh: 老板告诉我们不要为小错误大惊小怪。

examples.en: I think the project manager was fussing over the project timeline trying to make sure all the tasks were completed on time
examples.zh: 我觉得项目经理对项目时间表大惊小怪，试图确保所有任务都按时完成。

examples.en: I've decided to break off my partnership with him because of his unethical business practices
examples.zh: 因他不道德的商业行为，我决定终止与他的合作关系。

examples.en: The team decided to break off the development of the application feature due to technical challenges
examples.zh: 团队决定因技术挑战终止了这个应用的开发。

title: Lola 外企职场英语课程 第四课

phrases.definition_zh: 加倍努力；超越预期，付出额外的努力

examples.en: If you want to stand out in your job you need to be willing to go the extra mile to provide exceptional service to your customers
examples.zh: 如果你想在工作中脱颖而出，你需要愿意尽一切努力为客户提供卓越的服务。

examples.en: As a salesperson, it's important to go the extra mile to make sure your clients are satisfied with their purchase
examples.zh: 作为一名销售人员，付出额外努力以确保客户对购买满意是非常重要的。

phrases.definition_zh: 中止计划；中止某项活动

examples.en: After months of planning we had to pull the plug on the project due to budget constraints
examples.zh: 经过数月的计划，由于预算限制，我们不得不中止这个项目。

examples.en: With the company facing financial difficulties the board of directors decided to pull the plug and shutdown operations
examples.zh: 由于公司面临财务困境，董事会决定停止营运并关闭公司。

phrases.definition_zh: 触及痛处；触及敏感的地方

examples.en: The comment about her performance touched a nerve and she became defensive
examples.zh: 有关她表现的评论触及了她的痛处，她开始防御。

examples.en: The proposal to cut benefits touched a nerve with the union members
examples.zh: 削减福利的提议触及了工会成员的痛处。

examples.en: The decision about the new project is still up in the air
examples.zh: 关于新项目的决定仍未确定。

examples.en: The exact date for the meeting is still up in the air
examples.zh: 会议的确切日期还未确定。

examples.en: We need to come up with a new marketing strategy that will move the needle and increase sales
examples.zh: 我们需要提出一种新的市场营销策略，可以改变现状并增加销售额。

examples.en: Investing in new technology can move the needle and give us a competitive edge
examples.zh: 投资于新技术，可以推动变革并为我们赢得竞争优势。

examples.en: A strong social media presence can move the needle and help us reach a wider audience
examples.zh: 一个强大的社交媒体存在可以改变现状并帮助我们吸引更广泛的受众。

phrases.definition_zh: 批准某人做某事；给某人开绿灯

examples.en: Our boss finally gave us the green light to start the new project
examples.zh: 我们的老板终于批准我们开始新项目了。

examples.en: The CEO gave the marketing department the green light to launch the new ad campaign
examples.zh: CEO给了市场部门批准，让他们推出新的广告宣传活动。

phrases.definition_zh: 在...情形下；在...之中，表示某人或某事处于某种情况或环境之中

examples.en: In the midst of a crisis it's important to stay calm and focused
examples.zh: 在危机中，保持冷静和专注非常重要。

examples.en: In the midst of the project we realized that we needed to make some changes to the timeline
examples.zh: 在项目进行中，我们意识到需要对时间表进行一些更改。

phrases.definition_zh: 开始工作；开始行动；打开局面

examples.en: Let's get the ball rolling
examples.zh: 让我们开始行动吧！

examples.en: Once we get the ball rolling things should start moving quickly
examples.zh: 一旦我们开始行动，事情就应该快速推进了。

examples.en: Let's get the ball rolling on this project and start gathering ideas
examples.zh: 让我们开始行动吧，收集这个项目的想法。

phrases.definition_zh: 全面考虑；确保万无一失

examples.en: Before we launch the new product we need to make sure we cover all the bases
examples.zh: 在我们推出新产品之前，我们需要确保万无一失。

examples.en: The team is working hard to cover all the bases for the upcoming project
examples.zh: 团队正在努力全面考虑即将到来的项目。

examples.en: I'll review the proposal again to make sure we've covered all the bases
examples.zh: 我会再次审查提案，以确保我们已经全面考虑了。

phrases.definition_zh: 使事情顺利进行；保持进展顺利；保持计划正常进行

examples.en: As a project manager it's your job to keep things on track and make sure everyone is meeting their deadlines
examples.zh: 作为项目经理，你的工作是使事情顺利进行，确保每个人都能够按时完成任务。

examples.en: Let's have a quick check in meeting to make sure we're keeping things on track
examples.zh: 让我们进行一个快速的会议，确保我们使事情顺利进行。

phrases.definition_zh: 重归正轨;重新回到正常的轨道上

examples.en: The company's financial situation has taken a hit but we're taking steps to get back on track
examples.zh: 公司的财务状况受到了打击，但我们正在采取措施重新回到正常轨道上。

examples.en: The team had some setbacks but we're confident we can get back on track and deliver a quality product

phrases.definition_zh: 原点；重新开始；从头开始; 一夜回到解放前

examples.en: Let's start with square one and work our way up
examples.zh: 让我们从头开始，一步步来。

examples.en: We need to go back to square one and rethink our approach
examples.zh: 我们需要回到起点，重新思考我们的方法。

examples.en: We'll have to start from square one since the previous plan fell through
examples.zh: 由于之前的计划失败了，我们将不得不重新开始。

examples.en: Our team worked hard on the project but the results were not what we were hoping for. We need to go back to square one and figure out where we went wrong
examples.zh: 我们团队为这个项目付出了很多努力，但结果并不如我们所愿。我们需要回到起点，找出问题所在。

phrases.definition_zh: 重新开始；从头再来；重起炉灶

examples.en: We thought we had a winning proposal, but the client rejected it, so it's back to the drawing board
examples.zh: 我们原本以为这个提案很有胜算，但客户拒绝了，所以我们只能重新开始。

examples.en: I just wanted to touch base with you about the project and make sure we're still on track
examples.zh: 我只是想跟你联系一下关于这个项目，确保我们还在正确的轨道上。

examples.en: Let's touch base later this week to discuss our progress
examples.zh: 我们本周晚些时候再联系，讨论我们的进展情况。

examples.en: We're currently short staffed so everyone needs to pitch in and help out as much as possible
examples.zh: 我们现在人手不足，所以每个人都需要尽可能地合作帮忙。

examples.en: We're currently short staffed and need to recruit more employees
examples.zh: 我们目前人手不足，需要招聘更多员工。

examples.en: We don't have any templates for this project so we'll have to start from scratch
examples.zh: 我们没有任何模板可用于这个项目，所以我们必须从零开始。

examples.en: The client wants a unique design for their website so we'll have to create it from scratch
examples.zh: 客户想要一个独特的网站设计，所以我们必须从零开始创造。

examples.en: Our team has to develop a new product from scratch which will take a lot of time and effort
examples.zh: 我们的团队必须从零开始开发一款新产品，这需要很多时间和努力。

examples.en: I know you're in favor of the new proposal but let me play devil's advocate for a moment and point out some potential flaws
examples.zh: 我知道你支持这个新的提议，但是让我充当反对者的角色，指出一些潜在的缺陷。

examples.en: In order to make sure we have a well rounded decision we should assign someone to play devil's advocate during the meeting
examples.zh: 为了确保我们做出一个全面的决定，我们应该在会议上指定一个人来扮演反对者的角色。

examples.en: As far as things on my end everything is going smoothly
examples.zh: 就我而言，一切都很顺利。

examples.en: On my end I'm still waiting for some information from the client
examples.zh: 就我而言，我还在等待客户提供一些信息。

examples.en: We need to come up with some actionable steps to improve our customer service
examples.zh: 我们需要想出一些可行的步骤来改善我们的客户服务。

examples.en: Your feedback is very helpful but can you give us some more actionable suggestions
examples.zh: 你的反馈很有帮助，但是你能给我们一些更具体的建议吗？

examples.en: The kick off meeting for the new project is scheduled for next Monday
examples.zh: 新项目的启动会议定于下周一举行。

examples.en: In the kick off meeting we'll go over the project scope timeline and goals
examples.zh: 在启动会议上，我们将讨论项目范围、时间表和目标。

phrases.definition_zh: 一个显而易见的、但被忽略或避免谈论的问题、挑战或难题；这个问题通常被认为是敏感、难以解决或可能引起尴尬或不愉快的局面，因此被人们避免提及或讨论。

examples.en: We all know there's an elephant in the room but no one wants to talk about the fact that the project is behind schedule
examples.zh: 我们都知道有一个显而易见的问题，但是没有人愿意谈论这个项目进度落后的事实。

examples.en: The financial struggles of the company are the elephant in the room that no one wants to acknowledge
examples.zh: 公司的财务困境是存在的显而易见的问题，但没有人愿意承认。

examples.en: We need to engage in forward planning to identify potential challenges and opportunities that may arise in the future
examples.zh: 我们需要进行前瞻性规划，以确定未来可能出现的挑战和机遇。

examples.en: Forward planning is essential for a successful product launch, as it ensures that we have the necessary resources and strategies in place
examples.zh: 前瞻性规划对于成功的产品推出至关重要，因为它确保我们有必要的资源和策略。

phrases.definition_zh: 未来某个时间；在进程的后面阶段

examples.en: We may not see the benefits of this investment right away but down the line it will pay off
examples.zh: 我们可能不会立即看到这个投资的好处，但最终会得到回报。

examples.en: We need to make sure our decisions are sustainable and will hold up down the line
examples.zh: 我们需要确保我们的决策是可持续的，可以在未来长期有效。

phrases.definition_zh: 只触及到表面；只是表面上的了解或探索，没有深入挖掘或探究问题的；本质或细节

examples.en: I've only had a chance to scratch the surface of the project so I'll need some time to dive deeper and understand the details
examples.zh: 我只是初步了解了一下这个项目，所以需要一些时间来深入了解细节。

examples.en: The training session was helpful but we only scratched the surface of what the software can do
examples.zh: 这次培训很有帮助，但我们只是初步了解了一下软件的功能。

phrases.definition_zh: 一个大概的估计或范围；相当于 rough estimate 或 approximate figure

examples.en: Can you give me a ballpark figure of how much this project is going to cost
examples.zh: 你能给我一个大概的估算，这个项目要花多少钱吗？

examples.en: I'm not sure of the exact number but I would say the revenue for this quarter is in the ballpark of 500 000
examples.zh: 我不确定具体数字，但我会说本季度的收入大概在50万美元左右。

phrases.definition_zh: 没有晋升机会、没有前途、没有发展空间的工作

examples.en: I'm tired of this dead end job with no opportunities for growth or advancement
examples.zh: 我厌倦了这个没有任何成长和晋升机会的死胡同工作。

examples.en: I need to find a way out of this dead end job and start pursuing my career goals
examples.zh: 我需要找到一条走出这个死胡同工作、开始追求自己职业目标的路。

phrases.phrase: Keep your eye on the ball
phrases.definition_zh: 保持注意力集中；注意力要集中在重点上

examples.en: We have a tight deadline for this project so let's keep our eye on the ball and focus on delivering the key features first
examples.zh: 这个项目的截止日期很紧，所以让我们保持注意力集中，专注于先完成关键功能。

examples.en: It's easy to get distracted by other projects but we need to keep our eye on the ball and prioritize the tasks that will have the biggest impact on our business
examples.zh: 很容易被其他项目分心，但是我们需要保持注意力集中，优先处理对我们业务影响最大的任务。

phrases.definition_zh: 亏损中；负债；赤字；是一个常用的商业术语，通常指企业或个人财务状况不佳，负债或亏损。

examples.en: We need to cut expenses because we're currently in the red
examples.zh: 我们需要削减开支，因为我们现在处于亏损状态。

examples.en: The company has been in the red for the first two quarters this year
examples.zh: 公司今年前两个季度一直处于亏损状态。

examples.en: Thanks to our cost cutting measures we are finally in the black again
examples.zh: 多亏了我们的成本削减措施，我们终于重新实现盈利了。

examples.en: We need to come up with a solid business plan to ensure we stay in the black
examples.zh: 我们需要制定一个坚实的商业计划，以确保我们继续保持盈利状态。

examples.en: The last project was great but we need to raise the bar for the next one and aim for even better results
examples.zh: 上一个项目做得很好，但我们需要提高下一个项目的标准，争取更好的结果。

examples.en: We need to raise the bar in terms of customer service if we want to remain competitive in this market
examples.zh: 如果我们想在这个市场上保持竞争力，我们需要在客户服务方面提高要求。

phrases.definition_zh: 启动或开始一项工作或计划

examples.en: We need to get this project off the ground before the end of the quarter
examples.zh: 我们需要在季度结束之前启动这个项目。

examples.en: The team has been working hard to get the new product off the ground and we're excited to launch it soon
examples.zh: 团队一直在努力启动新产品，我们很快就会推出它。

phrases.definition_zh: 为某个项目或工作贡献自己的专业知识、技能、资源或其他有价值的东西

examples.en: We need team members who can bring new ideas and fresh perspectives to the table
examples.zh: 我们需要团队成员能够为项目带来新的想法和新鲜的视角.

examples.en: We are looking for a partner who can bring funding and expertise to the table to help us grow our business
examples.zh: 我们正在寻找一位合作伙伴，能够为我们带来资金和专业知识，帮助我们扩大业务。

examples.en: Let's keep this meeting short and sweet and focus on the key points
examples.zh: 让我们把会议简短明了，专注于要点。

examples.en: I'll give you the short and sweet version of the proposal and we can go over the details later if you're interested
examples.zh: 我会给你简短明了的提案版本，如果你有兴趣，我们可以稍后再讨论细节。

examples.en: The presentation was short and sweet but it effectively conveyed the message
examples.zh: 演示文稿简短明了，但它有效地传达了信息。

phrases.definition_zh: 让某人了解某个情况或进展，并随时更新他们任何重要信息

examples.en: Please keep me in the loop about any changes to the project timeline
examples.zh: 请让我了解项目时间表的任何更改情况。

examples.en: I'll keep you in the loop on the new product launch so you're up to date on our progress
examples.zh: 我会让你了解我们新产品的推出情况，这样你就能及时了解我们的进展。

examples.en: We want to make sure all team members are kept in the loop so we'll send out regular project updates
examples.zh: 我们想确保所有团队成员都得到及时信息，所以我们会定期发送项目更新.

phrases.phrase: Hit the nail on the head
phrases.definition_zh: 一针见血; 说到点子; 很精准；很到位 =spoton；这个短语常用于表示某人或某物成功地解决了问题或准确地描述了某个情况或问题的核心。它也可以用于表扬某人的判断力或建议的精准性。

examples.en: I think your proposal hits the nail on the head and addresses all of our concerns
examples.zh: 我认为你的建议很到位，能够解决我们所有的问题。

examples.en: We need to hit the nail on the head and come up with a solution that works for everyone
examples.zh: 我们需要精准地解决问题，找到一种对所有人都有效的解决方案。

examples.en: The new marketing campaign really hit the nail on the head and generated a lot of buzz
examples.zh: 新的营销活动确实很成功，引起了很多关注。

phrases.definition_zh: 想法一致；这个短语通常用于描述人们之间的协作和沟通，表示他们在想法、目标或计划方面达成了一致。它强调了共同理解和对话的重要性，以确保所有人都理解和支持团队的共同目标。

examples.en: Let's have a meeting to discuss the project and make sure we're all on the same page
examples.zh: 让我们开个会议来讨论项目，并确保我们都想法一致。

examples.en: I'm glad we're on the same page about the direction we want to take the company
examples.zh: 我很高兴我们在公司发展方向上想法一致。

phrases.definition_zh: 跳出思维定式；打破常规思维；这个短语通常用于鼓励人们尝试不同的方法或思考方式来解决问题或达到目标。它强调了创新、探索和冒险精神的重要性，以获得新的想法和解决方案。

examples.en: Let's think outside the box and come up with a creative solution to this problem
examples.zh: 让我们放开思维，想出一个创意解决方案。

examples.en: We need to think outside the box to attract new customers and stand out from our competitors
examples.zh: 我们需要有创意地吸引新客户，从竞争对手中脱颖而出。

examples.en: Instead of following the same old strategy let's think outside the box and try something new
examples.zh: 不要再按照老套路走，让我们放开思维，尝试一些新的方法。

phrases.definition_zh: 为了安全起见；为保险起见；用在工作中表示为了避免风险或事故发生，采取预防措施或者保守做法。

examples.en: Let's double check the figures to be safe before submitting the report
examples.zh: 为保险起见，在提交报告之前，让我们仔细核对数字。

examples.en: To be safe we should have a back up plan in case the main one fails
examples.zh: 为了安全起见，如果主计划失败，我们应该有备用计划。

phrases.definition_zh: 保持领先优势；走在前沿；表示比其他人更早地了解和采用新的技术、趋势、思想或策略，以在市场竞争中保持领先地位。常用于商业和管理领域。

examples.en: To stay ahead of the curve we need to constantly innovate and adapt to changing market trends
examples.zh: 为了保持领先，我们需要不断创新和适应市场变化。

examples.en: We're always looking for ways to improve our products and services so we can stay ahead of the curve
examples.zh: 我们一直在寻找改进产品和服务的方法，以便保持领先地位。

examples.en: In this industry you need to stay ahead of the curve if you want to succeed
examples.zh: 在这个行业，如果你想成功，就必须跑在前面。

examples.en: It's important to find ways to juggle work and home life to avoid burnout
examples.zh: 为避免过度疲劳，找到平衡工作和家庭生活的方法非常重要。

examples.en: Juggling work and home life can be challenging but with good time management skills it's possible
examples.zh: 平衡工作和家庭生活可能会有困难，但通过良好的时间管理技巧，是有可能做到的。

phrases.definition_zh: 混乱没有逻辑；到处都是

examples.en: All over the place
examples.zh: 在工作场合中通常指的是事情或思路杂乱无章、缺乏条理、不集中。可以翻译为“乱七八糟”、“一团糟”、“东一块、西一块”等表达方式。

examples.en: Her work is all over the place she needs to focus and prioritize her tasks
examples.zh: 她的工作杂乱无章，她需要集中精力并按照优先级完成任务。

examples.en: The meeting was all over the place we didn't make any progress
examples.zh: 会议讨论跑题了，我们没有取得任何进展。

phrases.definition_zh: 言归正传；直入主题；这个短语通常用来表示停止闲扯或者不必要的废话，直接进入主题或关键问题。

examples.en: Can you cut to the chase and give me the bottom line on this project
examples.zh: 你能不能直接点，给我这个项目的结论？

examples.en: I appreciate your detailed explanation but let's cut to the chase and discuss the solutions we can offer to the client
examples.zh: 我很感激你的详细解释，但是让我们直接进入主题，讨论我们能够向客户提供的解决方案。

examples.en: Time is running out let's cut to the chase and finalize the budget for this quarter
examples.zh: 时间不多了，我们来赶紧确定这个季度的预算吧。

examples.en: Can you give me the key points of your proposal in a nutshell
examples.zh: 你能否简单地概括一下你的提案要点？

examples.en: In a nutshell we need to cut costs and increase revenue to improve our profitability
examples.zh: 简言之，我们需要削减成本并增加收入以提高盈利能力。

examples.en: The report in a nutshell shows that our sales have increased by 15 compared to last year
examples.zh: 简单来说，这份报告显示我们的销售额较去年增长了15%。

examples.en: If that's the case we need to revise our strategy accordingly
examples.zh: 如果是这样的话，我们需要相应地修改我们的策略。

title: Lola 外企职场英语课程 第五课

phrases.definition_zh: 铺天盖地地去做某件事；过于雄心勃勃地去做某件事；不切实际；这个短语通常用来形容某个计划或任务过于庞大、复杂、不切实际，难以完成或浪费时间和资源。

examples.en: We can't boil the ocean Let's focus on solving the most critical issues first
examples.zh: 我们不能不切实际的做事。让我们先集中精力解决最关键的问题。

examples.en: We don't have time to boil the ocean Let's prioritize and tackle the most urgent tasks first
examples.zh: 我们没有时间去不切实际的做大事。让我们先优先考虑并处理最紧急的任务。

examples.en: We need to be realistic and avoid trying to boil the ocean Let's set achievable goals and work towards them
examples.zh: 我们需要实事求是，避免不切实际。让我们制定可实现的目标并朝着它们努力。

phrases.definition_zh: 激发...的最佳潜力；发挥...的最大优势；让...尽力达到最好；；这个短语通常用于形容某种方法、策略或者领导风格，能够最大化发挥某个人、团队或事物的潜力和能力，使其达到最佳的表现和成果。

examples.en: Our goal is to bring out the best in our product and make it stand out in the market
examples.zh: 我们的目标是发挥产品的最大优势，在市场中脱颖而出。

examples.en: Effective communication can bring out the best in a team
examples.zh: 有效的沟通可以发掘团队的最大潜力。

examples.en: We want to bring out the best in our employees by providing them with opportunities for growth and development
examples.zh: 我们想通过为员工提供成长和发展的机会来发掘他们的潜力。

phrases.definition_zh: 显而易见的决定；不需思考的决策；这个短语通常用来形容一种非常明显或者容易的决策或选择，无需花费太多时间和精力就能做出决定。

examples.en: Choosing the cheaper option was a no brainer for us
examples.zh: 选择更便宜的选项对我们来说是轻而易举的。

examples.en: It's a no brainer to invest in employee training and development
examples.zh: 在员工培训和发展方面进行投资是显而易见的。

examples.en: Hiring the experienced candidate was a no brainer decision
examples.zh: 雇用经验丰富的候选人是一个显而易见的决定。

phrases.definition_zh: 深陷困境，进退两难，忙乱不堪

examples.en: In the weeds
examples.zh: 是一个常用的英语商业术语，通常指一个人或团队正在经历一些困难或障碍，无法有效地处理任务或问题。中文翻译可以是"陷入困境"，"处于困难境地"，或"进退两难"。

examples.en: We're really busy right now with a lot of customer complaints so we're a bit in the weeds
examples.zh: 我们现在非常忙，有很多客户投诉，所以我们有点陷入困境。

examples.en: I was hoping to finish this project on time but some unexpected issues came up and now I'm a little in the weeds
examples.zh: 我本来想按时完成这个项目，但是突然出现了一些问题，我现在有点忙不过来了。

examples.en: We need to hire more employees to help us because we're really in the weeds now
examples.zh: 我们需要多招一些员工来帮忙，因为我们已经深陷困境了。

phrases.definition_zh: 挑拨离间；制造分歧；这个短语常常用于形容通过煽动、挑拨或者制造隔阂来达到某个目的的行为。

examples.en: He's trying to drive a wedge between us by spreading rumors and talking negatively about others to achieve his own goals
examples.zh: 他试图通过制造谣言和说长道短来挑拨我们之间的关系，从而实现他自己的目的。

examples.en: If we don't pay attention to those who are trying to drive a wedge our team's collaboration will be compromised
examples.zh: 如果我们不注意到那些试图制造分歧的人，我们的团队合作关系将会被破坏。

examples.en: The limited budget poses a problem for our marketing strategy
examples.zh: 有限的预算对我们的市场营销策略构成了难题。

examples.en: The outdated technology is posing a problem for our team's efficiency
examples.zh: 过时的技术正在对我们团队的效率构成问题。

phrases.definition_zh: 通常用来形容一个任务、项目或计划的各个复杂组成部分，它们可能会因为某些因素而；随时发生变化，因此整个项目的成功或失败取决于如何管理这些不确定因素。

examples.en: The project has a lot of moving parts so it's hard to predict how it will turnout
examples.zh: 这个项目有许多活动部分，所以很难预测它会如何发展。

examples.en: We need to be prepared for unexpected changes because there are so many moving parts in this plan
examples.zh: 我们需要为意外的变化做好准备，因为这个计划有太多复杂的组成部分.

phrases.definition_zh: 是指采取强硬手段来达到自己的目的，通常意味着不妥协、不让步，甚至有可能使用威；胁或恐吓等手段来实现自己的利益。这个短语通常用来描述商业谈判、竞争或争端中的行为方式。

examples.en: The negotiations have stalled and I think it's time to start playing hardball
examples.zh: 谈判陷入僵局，我认为是时候开始采取强硬手段了。

examples.en: If they won't negotiate we'll have to play hardball and threaten legal action
examples.zh: 如果他们不肯谈判，我们就必须采取强硬手段，威胁采取法律行动。

examples.en: Our company needs to start playing hardball if we want to stay competitive in this industry
examples.zh: 如果我们想在这个行业保持竞争力，我们的公司需要开始采取强硬手段。

phrases.definition_zh: 试水；表示试探或测试某个计划或想法的可行性。

examples.en: We're testing the waters with this new product to see if it will be successful
examples.zh: 我们正在试水这个新产品，看它是否会成功。

examples.en: Before committing to a new marketing strategy let's test the water first by running a small campaign
examples.zh: 在决定新的营销策略前，我们先试探一下，进行一个小规模的推广活动。

phrases.definition_zh: 精通的；熟练的；有经验的；懂行的；常用于形容某个人在某个领域或行业中有深刻的理解和经验

examples.en: She's really tech savvy and can troubleshoot any computer problem
examples.zh: 她非常擅长技术，并且能够解决任何电脑问题。

examples.en: Our new sales manager is quite business savvy and has already helped us increase revenue
examples.zh: 我们的新销售经理非常精通商业，并且已经帮助我们增加了收入。

examples.en: He's media savvy and knows how to get the company's message across
examples.zh: 他精通媒体，知道如何传达公司的信息。

phrases.phrase: The ball is in your court
phrases.definition_zh: 该轮到你了；你该采取行动了；意思是告诉对方现在需要他们采取下一步行动或决策，而不是由自己来做。这个短语通常用于告诉对方他们需要负起责任并采取行动来推进工作进程。它也可以用于表达一种等待对方回应的状态，表示现在需要对方做出反应或采取行动。

examples.en: I've done everything I can to prepare the report now the ball is in your court to review it and give me your feedback
examples.zh: 我已经尽我所能准备好了报告，现在该轮到你审查它并给我你的反馈了。

examples.en: We have provided all the necessary materials and now the ball is in your court
examples.zh: 我们已经提供了所有必要的材料，现在球在你们的场内了。

examples.en: We have done our part and now it's your turn The ball is in your court
examples.zh: 我们已经完成了自己的部分，现在轮到你们了。

examples.en: We're all set for the meeting tomorrow
examples.zh: 我们明天的会议已经准备妥当了。

examples.en: The project is all set and ready to launch
examples.zh: 这个项目已经准备就绪，随时可以启动了。

examples.en: Don't worry I'm all set for the presentation
examples.zh: 别担心，我已经为演讲做好了充分准备。

phrases.definition_zh: 全局；指需要从全局的角度看待事情，关注更广阔、更长远的利益和发展方向。

examples.en: We need to take a higher level view grasp the big picture and see the overall picture of this project
examples.zh: 我们需要站在更高的层面，把握全局，看清楚这个项目的大局。

examples.en: We can't just focus on the details we also need to keep the big picture of the project's plan and goals in mind
examples.zh: 我们不能仅仅关注细节，也要关注项目整体的规划和目标。

phrases.definition_zh: 厚脸皮；通常用来形容一个人能够承受各种挑战和批评，不会因此感到过于受伤或难过的心理状态。

examples.en: If you want to make it in this industry you need to have thick skin
examples.zh: 要想在这个行业立足，你必须得有一副厚脸皮。

examples.en: We need to be able to face criticism and challenges with resilience, and only those with thick skin will be able to succeed
examples.zh: 我们需要能够坚定地面对批评和挑战，只有那些具有厚皮肤的人才能够成功。

phrases.definition_zh: 放弃，认输；表示他或她已经决定放弃或认输了。这个短语通常用来形容一个人在面对挑战或困难时，觉得自己无法继续下去，或者认为自己已经尽力而为，但仍然失败

examples.en: I've been trying to fix this problem for hours but I think it's time to throw in the towel
examples.zh: 我已经试图解决这个问题好几个小时了，但是我想现在是放弃的时候了。

examples.en: After months of negotiations we've decided to throw in the towel and walk away from the deal
examples.zh: 经过数月的谈判，我们决定放弃并退出这笔交易。

phrases.phrase: Off the top of your head
phrases.definition_zh: 随口一说；毫无准备地说出；通常指的是说话者不做任何准备或思考就直接表达出自己的想法、意见或答案。在工作场合中，这个短语通常用来形容一个人快速地、凭直觉地回答问题，而不是经过仔细的分析或研究。

examples.en: What's the revenue projection for next quarter Off the top of my head I would say around 500 000
examples.zh: 下个季度的收入预期是多少？随口一说，我觉得大概会在50万左右。

examples.en: How much time do we have left to finish this project? Off the top of my head, I would say we have about two weeks
examples.zh: 我们还有多少时间完成这个项目？随口一说，我觉得我们还有大约两个星期的时间。

phrases.definition_zh: 派上用场；表示某个事物在某个场合或情况下会非常有用或有帮助。

examples.en: Having a good knowledge of data analysis will come in handy when working on this project
examples.zh: 熟练掌握数据分析知识在处理这个项目时会派上用场。

examples.en: Keeping an updated contact list of industry experts will come in handy when we need to reach out for advice
examples.zh: 及时更新行业专家的联系列表，将在我们需要寻求建议时非常有用。

examples.en: Understanding different cultural practices and customs will come in handy when working with global teams
examples.zh: 当与全球团队合作时，了解不同文化惯例和习俗会派上用场。

phrases.definition_zh: 提出反对意见;故意唱反调;质疑不足之处；直译为“扮演魔鬼的代言人”，通常指在辩论或讨论中，故意提出异议或反对的观点，以引发更深入的讨论或思考。在职场中，也常用于指一些人在会议或讨论中提出不同意见，以促进全面考虑和辩论。

examples.en: I'm not trying to argue against your idea but let me play devil's advocate for a moment to see if we've thought of all the potential problems
examples.zh: 我不是在反对你的想法，但是我来提出些反对意见，看看我们是否考虑到了所有可能的问题。

examples.en: Before we make any final decisions let's have someone play devil's advocate to poke holes in our plan
examples.zh: 在我们做出最终决定之前，让人来指出不足之处，找出我们计划的漏洞。

examples.en: I'm afraid I dropped the ball on that project and now we're behind schedule
examples.zh: 恐怕我在那个项目上失职了，现在我们进度落后了。

examples.en: She dropped the ball by forgetting to follow up with the client and now we may lose the account
examples.zh: 她由于忘记与客户跟进而失误了，现在我们可能会失去这个客户。

examples.en: Don't drop the ballon this presentation it's a big opportunity for us
examples.zh: 在这个演讲中不要出差错——这对我们来说是个重大的机会。

examples.en: After months of hard work our project has finally been brought to fruition
examples.zh: 经过数月的努力工作，我们的项目终于实现了。

examples.en: The team worked tirelessly to bring the new product to fruition and it was well worth the effort
examples.zh: 团队不辞辛劳地工作，把新产品变成现实，一切的努力都是值得的。

phrases.phrase: Have a lot on one's plate
phrases.definition_zh: 有很多事情要处理；任务繁重

examples.en: I can't take on any more projects right now I already have a lot on my plate
examples.zh: 我现在不能再接受更多的项目，我已经很忙了。

examples.en: Sorry I can't attend the meeting today I have a lot on my plate and need to focus on completing these urgent tasks
examples.zh: 很抱歉我不能参加今天的会议，我有很多事情要做，需要集中精力完成这些紧急任务。

phrases.definition_zh: 被...淹没；通常表示某人被大量的事情、工作或信息淹没，无法有效地处理或应对。

examples.en: I'm swamped by emails it's hard to keep up with all the messages
examples.zh: 我的邮箱淹没在邮件中了，很难跟上所有的消息。

examples.en: The IT department is swamped by technical support requests from employees IT
examples.zh: IT部门被员工的技术支持请求淹没了。

phrases.definition_zh: 立即开始行动；快速上手工作；迅速进入状态；通常用来形容一个人在开始一个新工作或新项目时，能够立即开始行动并展现出高效率、高质量的工作表现。这个短语表示一个人能够立即适应新环境，快速上手工作，并能够做出具有积极影响的成果。

examples.en: Sarah hit the ground running in her new role as project manager
examples.zh: Sarah在她的新项目经理角色中立即开始行动。

examples.en: The team was able to hit the ground running thanks to their extensive preparation
examples.zh: 由于充分的准备，团队能够立即开始行动。

examples.en: John was determined to hit the ground running and quickly establish himself as a top performer
examples.zh: John决心立即开始行动，迅速证明自己是顶尖的绩效表现者。

phrases.definition_zh: 施展你的魔法；是一个口语化的短语，通常用来形容某个人的技能或才能能够产生非常出色的成果，就像魔法一样神奇。

examples.en: We need you to weave your magic and come up with a creative solution to this problem
examples.zh: 我们需要你施展你的魔法，提出一个有创意的解决方案来解决这个问题。

examples.en: The designer was able to weave her magic and create a stunning visual display for the product launch
examples.zh: 设计师施展她的魔法，为产品发布会创造了令人惊叹的视觉效果。

examples.en: Our forthcoming product launch is generating a lot of buzz in the industry
examples.zh: 我们即将发布的产品在业内引起了很大的关注。

examples.en: The team is busy preparing for the forthcoming audit
examples.zh: 团队正在忙于为即将到来的审计做准备。

phrases.definition_zh: 陷入困境，惹上麻烦；通常用来形容某人或某团队遇到麻烦或处境困难

examples.en: The CEO is in hot water with the board of directors over his decision to merge with a competitor
examples.zh: CEO因其与竞争对手合并的决定而与董事会闹得不愉快。

examples.en: The sales team is in hot water after losing a major account due to poor customer service
examples.zh: 销售团队因客户服务不周导致失去一个重要客户而陷入麻烦。

phrases.definition_zh: 及时地；在最后一刻；在最后关头

examples.en: I was able to submit the report in the nick of time just a few minutes before the deadline
examples.zh: 我在最后关头赶在最后期限前提交了报告。

examples.en: The IT team was able to fix the issue in the nick of time before the system crashed
examples.zh: IT团队在系统崩溃之前刚好能够解决问题。

examples.en: I submitted my proposal just in the nick of time before the submission deadline
examples.zh: 在截止日期前的最后一刻，我成功提交了我的建议书。

phrases.definition_zh: 经历过；具备，掌握；已获得某物；通常表示某人已经具备了某种技能、经验或完成了某项任务

examples.en: He has years of experience under his belt and is a valuable asset to the team
examples.zh: 他有多年的经验，是团队中非常有价值的人才。

examples.en: Having a few coding languages under your belt is essential in the tech industry
examples.zh: 掌握几种编程语言对科技行业来说非常重要。

phrases.definition_zh: 灵光一闪；恍然大悟；豁然开朗；顿悟时刻；通常指某个人突然明白了一件事情或发现了一个解决问题的关键点

examples.en: The designer had an a ha moment when she realized that a small design tweak could significantly improve the user experience
examples.zh: 设计师有了一个顿悟时刻，意识到微调设计可以显著改善用户体验。

examples.en: When the sales team saw the market data they had an a ha moment and realized that they needed to change their approach to reach their target audience
examples.zh: 当销售团队看到市场数据时，他们恍然大悟，意识到他们需要改变方法才能达到目标受众。

phrases.phrase: Take it to the next level
phrases.definition_zh: 进一步提高、达到更高水平、向更高层次发展；这个短语经常用来形容需要采取新措施、创新、改进或者扩展业务，以达到更高的目标。

examples.en: The CEO wants us to take our company's sales performance to the next level by exploring new markets and developing new products
examples.zh: CEO希望我们通过探索新市场和开发新产品，提高公司的销售业绩。

phrases.definition_zh: 导致某个决定、行动、解雇等最终实施的最后一击

examples.en: My boss was always criticizing me but when he blamed me for something I didn't even do that was the last straw I decided to quit
examples.zh: 我的老板总是批评我，但当他因为我根本没做的事情责怪我时，那是最后一根稻草。我决定辞职。

examples.en: We were willing to overlook her lack of experience but when she made a major mistake that cost us a lot of money that was the last straw
examples.zh: 我们可以容忍她缺乏经验，但当她犯下了一个导致我们损失巨大的重大错误时，那就是最后的导火索。

examples.en: As a manager you need to be accountable for the team's performance
examples.zh: 作为经理，你需要对团队的绩效负责。

examples.en: We must be accountable for delivering the project on time and within budget
examples.zh: 我们必须对按时并在预算内完成项目负责。

phrases.definition_zh: 遇到瓶颈期；达到瓶颈；停滞不前

examples.en: Our sales figures have hit a plateau and we need to find new strategies to increase revenue
examples.zh: 我们的销售数字已经达到了瓶颈，我们需要找到新的策略来增加收入。

examples.en: The company's growth has hit a plateau and we need to come up with fresh ideas to revitalize it
examples.zh: 公司的增长已经达到了瓶颈，我们需要想出新的点子来重新振兴它。

examples.en: The competition was stiff but we managed to win the contract
examples.zh: 竞争非常激烈，但我们设法赢得了合同。

examples.en: The boss gave me a stiff deadline so I'll have to work overtime to get it done
examples.zh: 老板给了我一个紧迫的期限，所以我必须加班来完成它。

examples.en: Can you give me the inside scoop on the new product launch
examples.zh: 你能给我关于新产品发布的内幕消息吗？

examples.en: I got the inside scoop on the company's plans for expansion
examples.zh: 我获得了公司扩张计划的内部消息。

examples.en: Getting the inside scoop on the competition is key to staying ahead in the market
examples.zh: 获取有关竞争对手的内幕消息是保持市场领先地位的关键。

phrases.definition_zh: 保持势头；保持上升势头

examples.en: We need to keep up the momentum on this project to ensure it's completed on time
examples.zh: 我们需要继续保持这个项目的势头，以确保它按时完成。

examples.en: After a successful launch we must keep up the momentum to maintain our market position
examples.zh: 在成功推出之后，我们必须保持势头，以保持我们的市场地位。

examples.en: We need to nip this issue in the bud before it turns into a major problem
examples.zh: 我们需要在问题恶化之前及时解决。

examples.en: It's important to nip negative behavior in the bud before it becomes a habit
examples.zh: 在负面行为形成习惯之前，及时制止非常重要。

examples.en: I'm afraid I won't be able to attend the meeting due to a tight schedule
examples.zh: 由于时间安排紧张，恐怕我无法参加会议。

examples.en: Our team is facing a tight schedule for the upcoming launch but we're confident we can pull it off
examples.zh: 我们的团队在即将推出的产品上面临着紧张的时间安排，但我们有信心能够完成。

phrases.definition_zh: 全面的；整体性的；综合的

examples.en: We need to take a holistic view of this project and consider the long term impacts on the business not just the short term benefits
examples.zh: 我们需要全面考虑这个项目，考虑对业务的长期影响，而不仅仅是短期利益。

examples.en: Our company's training program is designed to provide employees with a holistic understanding of our business operations and goals
examples.zh: 我们公司的培训计划旨在为员工提供对我们的业务运营和目标的全面理解。

phrases.definition_zh: 重点表扬；向...喊话，向...致敬

examples.en: Shout out to our customer service team for going above and beyond to help our customers
examples.zh: 向我们的客户服务团队喊话，感谢他们超越职责来帮助我们的客户。

examples.en: Shout out to our sales team for meeting and exceeding their targets this quarter
examples.zh: 向我们的销售团队喊话，感谢他们在本季度达到并超过了他们的目标。

phrases.definition_zh: 关键性改变；重大变革；表示某个事物或行动能够改变现状、打破传统或引起重大影响。

examples.en: The new product is a game changer in the industry and is sure to revolutionize the way we work
examples.zh: 这款新产品是业界的颠覆者，一定会彻底改变我们的工作方式。

examples.en: The implementation of this new software system has been a game changer for our productivity
examples.zh: 这个新软件系统的实施是我们生产力的重大变革。

examples.en: Just a heads up the meeting time has been changed to 3 pm
examples.zh: 提醒一下，会议时间改到下午3点了。

examples.en: I thought I'd give you a heads up about the traffic on the way to the airport
examples.zh: 提前通知你一声，去机场的路上可能会有交通拥堵。

phrases.definition_zh: 看出言外之意/领会内涵

examples.en: She wasn't actually saying she wanted to break up but I could tell she was unhappy I had to read between the lines
examples.zh: 她并没有直接说她想分手，但我能感觉到她不开心。我不得不看出了她的言外之意。

examples.en: The boss's words may sound positive but you need to read between the lines to understand his true intentions
examples.zh: 老板的话可能听起来很积极，但你需要看出他真正的意图。

phrases.definition_zh: 从视线中消失/不再注意

examples.en: I haven't heard from him in weeks He seems to have dropped off my radar
examples.zh: 我好几周没收到他的消息了，他似乎已经从我的视线中消失了。

examples.en: After the initial excitement the project dropped off our radar and we forgot about it for a while
examples.zh: 刚开始很兴奋，但之后这个项目就从我们的视线中消失了，我们一度忘记了它。

title: Lola 外企职场英语课程 第六课

examples.en: I'm confident we can meet the deadline if we all work together
examples.zh: 如果我们都齐心协力，我相信我们能够完成这个截止日期。

examples.en: The designer worked through the night to meet the deadline for the new product launch
examples.zh: 设计师整夜加班，以便在新产品发布的截止日期前完成工作。

examples.en: The bank agreed to waive the overdraft fee as a one time courtesy
examples.zh: 银行同意免除透支费，作为一次特殊的恩惠。

examples.en: The company decided to waive the cancellation fee for customers affected by the hurricane
examples.zh: 公司决定免除受飓风影响的客户的取消费。

examples.en: I know you're busy but can you make time for a quick meeting this afternoon
examples.zh: 我知道你很忙，但你能否抽出时间在今天下午参加一个快速的会议？

examples.en: She always makes time for exercise no matter how busy her schedule is
examples.zh: 无论她的日程有多忙，她总是为锻炼腾出时间。

examples.en: You need to stay on top of the latest industry trends if you want to stay competitive
examples.zh: 如果你想保持竞争力，就需要掌握最新的行业趋势。

examples.en: Make sure you stay on top of your emails so you don't miss any important messages
examples.zh: 确保你掌握好你的电子邮件，这样你就不会错过任何重要的信息。

phrases.phrase: Off the top of one's head
phrases.definition_zh: 一下子想到；即兴地；随口说；脱口而出

examples.en: I can come up with a list of potential solutions off the top of my head but we'll need to discuss them further
examples.zh: 我可以从脑海中列出一份潜在的解决方案清单，但我们需要进一步讨论。

examples.en: I didn't have time to prepare so I'll just speak off the top of my head during the presentation
examples.zh: 我没有时间准备，所以在演示中我只能即兴发言。

examples.en: The manager focused on picking the low hanging fruit to show quick progress
examples.zh: 经理专注于解决那些容易解决的问题，以展示快速的进展。

examples.en: We should leverage the low hanging fruit opportunities to drive early successes
examples.zh: 我们应该利用那些容易解决的机会，推动早期的成功。

phrases.definition_zh: 推动进展；取得重大进展

examples.en: Implementing this new strategy has the potential to move the needle and improve our company's profitability
examples.zh: 实施这项新策略有可能推动我们公司的盈利能力，产生积极的影响。

examples.en: We have identified a few key areas where we can focus our efforts to move the needle and make significant improvements to our operations
examples.zh: 我们确定了几个关键领域，可以集中精力来促进发展，对我们的运营产生重大的改进。

examples.en: You need to get up to speed on the new project so we can start working on it together
examples.zh: 你需要熟悉新项目的情况，这样我们才能一起开始工作。

examples.en: I'll read the report to get up to speed on the latest developments
examples.zh: 我会阅读报告，了解最新的发展情况。

phrases.definition_zh: 岌岌可危；受到威胁；表示某事物或某种情况可能面临危险、风险或受到威胁的状态。

examples.en: There is a lot at stake in this negotiation so we need to be careful
examples.zh: 这次谈判涉及到很大的利益，所以我们需要小心。

examples.en: The company's reputation is at stake if we don't deliver on time
examples.zh: 如果我们不能按时交付，公司的声誉将受到威胁。

examples.en: I was really bummed out when I didn't get the job
examples.zh: 我没能得到这份工作，感到非常失落。

examples.en: She was bummed out for days after her dog died
examples.zh: 她的狗死后，她感到失落了好几天。

examples.en: Come to think of it I have a meeting at that time so I can't make it
examples.zh: 想起来了，那个时间我有一个会议，所以我不能去。

examples.en: Come to think of it I haven't seen her in years
examples.zh: 想起来了，我好几年没见过她了。

phrases.definition_zh: 学习曲线；指的是在学习和掌握新事物、新技能或新任务时所需经历的过程，通常包括一开始的陡峭阶段，随后逐渐变得平缓并掌握得更熟练的过程。这个词语常用于描述人们在工作中学习新技能或适应新环境时所经历的学习过程。

examples.en: I'm new to this job so I'm still on a learning curve
examples.zh: 我刚上这份工作，所以我还在学习中。

examples.en: There's always a learning curve when you start a new project
examples.zh: 开始新项目时总会有一定的学习曲线。

examples.en: The company is willing to bend over backwards to accommodate the customer's needs
examples.zh: 公司愿意尽最大努力来满足客户的需求。

examples.en: She bent over backwards to help her friend move to a new apartment
examples.zh: 她为帮助她的朋友搬到新公寓而竭尽全力。

examples.en: I'm going out on a limb here but I think we should invest in this new technology
examples.zh: 我要孤注一掷了，但我认为我们应该投资这项新技术。

examples.en: She went out on a limb and quit her job to start her own business
examples.zh: 她孤注一掷地辞掉了工作，开始自己的事业。

phrases.definition_zh: 未经雕琢的钻石；意为一个尚未被发现或充分发挥潜力的人或事物，可能在外表或表现上尚不完美，但在潜力或价值上有很高的可能性。这个词语通常用于形容那些具有潜力但尚未被充分开发的人才、项目、产品或机会。

examples.en: This startup may not have a polished product yet but it has the potential to become a diamond in the rough with the right resources and support
examples.zh: 这家初创公司可能还没有一个完善的产品，但如果有正确的资源和支持，它潜力无限。

phrases.definition_zh: 顿悟；突然明白；一切都变得清晰

examples.en: After struggling with the problem for a while suddenly the penny dropped and I found the solution
examples.zh: 在艰苦地努力解决问题一段时间后，突然灵光一现，我找到了解决办法。

examples.en: During the meeting John was confused about the concept but then the penny dropped and he grasped it
examples.zh: 在会议期间，约翰对这个概念感到困惑，但后来灵光一现，他明白了。

phrases.definition_zh: 在...之间权衡利弊；走钢丝

examples.en: As a manager, you have to walk a tightrope between meeting the needs of your employees and meeting the needs of the company
examples.zh: 作为经理，你必须在满足员工的需求和公司的需求之间权衡利弊。

examples.en: The politician is walking a tightrope between appeasing his base and reaching out to undecided voters
examples.zh: 这位政治家正在讨好他的支持者和争取未定选民之间权衡利弊。

examples.en: I have to walk on eggshells around my boss because she's very unpredictable
examples.zh: 我得小心翼翼地和我的老板打交道，因为她很难预测。

examples.en: We have to walk on eggshells around him because he's been in a bad mood all day
examples.zh: 我们得小心翼翼地和他打交道，因为他整天心情不好。

phrases.phrase: Get your foot in the door

examples.en: I'm hoping to get my foot in the door with this internship and eventually get hired full time
examples.zh: 我希望通过这个实习先从小做起，最终能够被全职雇佣。

examples.en: It's always a good idea to get your foot in the door at a company you want to work for
examples.zh: 在你想要工作的公司里先从小做起总是个好主意。

examples.en: We came up short on the project deadline and had to ask for an extension
examples.zh: 我们没有在项目的截止日期之前完成，不得不要求延期。

examples.en: She fell short of her fundraising goal by 500
examples.zh: 她的筹款目标差了500美元。

phrases.definition_zh: 控制资金；掌握财政大权

examples.en: As the CFO, she holds the purse strings of the company, making all the financial decisions
examples.zh: 作为首席财务官，她掌握着公司的财政大权，做出所有的财务决策。

examples.en: The project manager is the one who holds the purse strings for the project allocating budget and resources
examples.zh: 项目经理是这个项目的财务决策人员，负责分配预算和资源。

examples.en: I want to be the first one to submit my report so I need to beat my colleague to it
examples.zh: 我想要第一个提交报告，所以我需要先于我的同事完成。

examples.en: The company is trying to beat its competitors to the market with a new product
examples.zh: 公司正试图通过一种新产品比竞争对手更快进入市场。

phrases.definition_zh: 照章办事，按照规定的方式

examples.en: The company always does everything by the book to avoid any legal problems
examples.zh: 公司总是按照规定办事，以避免任何法律问题。

examples.en: As a teacher it is important to grade students' work by the book to ensure fairness
examples.zh: 作为一名教师，按照规定评分对于确保公平很重要。

phrases.phrase: Get off on the wrong foot
phrases.definition_zh: 一开始就出现问题，走错路

examples.en: I think we got off on the wrong foot in our meeting Let's start over and try to communicate more effectively
examples.zh: 我认为我们在会议上一开始就出了问题，让我们重新开始，试图更有效地沟通。

examples.en: It's important to make a good first impression on a new job to avoid getting off on the wrong foot with your boss or coworkers
examples.zh: 在新工作中留下良好的第一印象很重要，以避免与老板或同事走错路。

examples.en: He was caught red handed stealing company property from the supply room
examples.zh: 他在从办公用品室偷窃公司财产时被当场抓住现行。

examples.en: The employee was caught red handed manipulating data in the company's financial records
examples.zh: 这名员工在篡改公司财务记录时被当场抓住现行。

phrases.definition_zh: 十分普遍；不值一文；大量存在

examples.en: In the job market, entry-level positions for fresh graduates are a dime a dozen
examples.zh: 在就业市场上，针对新毕业生的初级职位非常普遍。

examples.en: There are so many freelancers offering similar services online, they are a dime a dozen
examples.zh: 在网上有很多提供类似服务的自由职业者，非常普遍。

phrases.phrase: Put the cart before the horse
phrases.definition_zh: 本末倒置，本不应先做的反而先做了

examples.en: They started hiring employees before even finalizing their business model. It's like putting the cart before the horse
examples.zh: 他们在连商业模式都没有最终确定的情况下就开始雇佣员工，这就是本末倒置。

examples.en: We can't start building the website without gathering the content and design requirements first. Let's not put the cart before the horse
examples.zh: 我们不能在先没有收集到内容和设计要求的情况下开始建设网站，不要本末倒置了。

phrases.definition_zh: 孤注一掷，不惜一切代价；放手一搏，不畏困难，追求最大的成功

examples.en: The company decided to go for broke and invest all their resources in the new project
examples.zh: 公司决定孤注一掷，将所有资源都投入到新项目中。

examples.en: We have a limited budget for this project but let's go for broke and invest in innovative solutions that can differentiate us from competitors
examples.zh: 我们在这个项目中的预算有限，但让我们全力以赴，投资于创新解决方案，从竞争对手中脱颖而出。

phrases.definition_zh: 用最少的投入得到最多的价值；在工作中通常用来表示在投资、花费资源或努力时获取最大回报的意思。

examples.en: Let's invest in training programs that offer the best bang for our buck in terms of employee development
examples.zh: 让我们投资于能够在员工发展方面获得最大回报的培训项目。

examples.en: As a startup we need to be strategic in our marketing efforts to get bang for our buck and maximize our limited budget
examples.zh: 作为初创企业，我们需要在营销方面有战略，以获得最大回报，充分发挥有限的预算。

phrases.definition_zh: 工作量很大，需要花费大量时间和精力

examples.en: The new manager has his work cut out for him to turn the company around
examples.zh: 新经理需要花费大量时间和精力来扭转公司的局面。

examples.en: The software developers have their work cut out for them to fix all the bugs in the latest release
examples.zh: 软件开发人员需要解决最新版本中的所有错误，他们的工作任务很艰巨。

phrases.definition_zh: 不是什么高深的技术，不难理解

examples.en: Learning how to use this software is not rocket science. It's actually quite user-friendly
examples.zh: 学习如何使用这个软件不是什么高深的技术，实际上非常易于使用。

examples.en: Don't worry about fixing the printer. It's not rocket science. Just follow the instructions
examples.zh: 不要担心修理打印机。这并不是什么高深的技术。只需要按照说明进行操作就可以了。

phrases.phrase: Get on the good side of someone
phrases.definition_zh: 获得某人的好感，赢得某人的支持

examples.en: It's important to get on the good side of your boss if you want to be promoted
examples.zh: 如果你想晋升，赢得你老板的好感很重要。

examples.en: The new employee quickly got on the good side of her coworkers by being friendly and helpful
examples.zh: 新员工通过友好和乐于助人很快赢得了同事的好感。

phrases.definition_zh: 听到（不可靠的）消息，获悉

examples.en: I got wind of a potential partnership opportunity with a major competitor during a networking event
examples.zh: 我在一个社交活动中听说了与一家重要竞争对手的潜在合作机会。

examples.en: She got wind of the upcoming lay off from a colleague before the official announcement
examples.zh: 她在官方公告之前从一位同事那里得知了即将到来的裁员计划。

examples.en: The restaurant went bust after only a year of operation due to poor management
examples.zh: 这家餐厅由于管理不善，在运营了一年后就破产了。

examples.en: The startup company went bust when it failed to secure additional funding
examples.zh: 这家初创公司未能获得额外的资金支持而破产。

examples.en: The company encouraged blue sky thinking among its employees in order to foster innovation
examples.zh: 公司鼓励员工进行创新思维，以促进创新。

examples.en: The team used blue sky thinking to come up with a completely new approach to the problem
examples.zh: 团队使用创新思维来提出完全新的解决问题的方法。

examples.en: Many companies are jumping on the bandwagon and implementing environmentally friendly policies
